info.description:
HTTP API for inspecting, querying, and searching stored Tapes sessions.

The REST surface exposes health checks, session listing and retrieval, derived session summaries, aggregate stats, semantic search, and a streamable MCP endpoint.
info.title: Tapes API

get.description: Returns a simple JSON string confirming that the API server is reachable.
Opens the streamable MCP endpoint for server-sent events. Stateless clients can use this to receive streamed MCP messages.

post.description:
Sends a JSON-RPC 2.0 request to the stateless Model Context Protocol endpoint mounted at /v1/mcp.

Typical calls include initialize, tools/list, and tools/call. When search is configured, the server exposes a search tool over this transport.

parameters.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 request
parameters.name: request

delete.description: Requests termination of a streamable MCP session when a client is using session-oriented transport semantics.

get.description: Embeds the query text, searches the configured vector store, and returns matching sessions with their full conversation branch.

parameters.description: Search query
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return
parameters.name: top_k

get.description: Embeds the query text and runs vector similarity over the embedded span projection (main llm spans, delta-only content). Each hit carries span, trace, and turn context.

parameters.description: Search query
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return
parameters.name: top_k

parameters.description: Tenant org UUID (defaults to the nil org)
parameters.name: X-Tapes-Org-Id

503.description: Span search is not configured or not yet initialized

get.description: Returns one row per harness session from the sessions table, newest first (last_seen_at desc), cursor-paginated. This is the product session view; the Merkle leaf-chain view lives at /v1/stems.

parameters.description: Maximum number of sessions to return (default 50, max 200)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous response
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Only include sessions active (last_seen_at) at or after this RFC3339 timestamp
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Only include sessions active (last_seen_at) before this RFC3339 timestamp
parameters.name: until

501.description: Sessions not supported by this backend

get.description: Returns a single session, the selected stem of turns, and a summary of every stem (root-to-leaf path) in the session. ?stem=longest (default) returns the longest stem; ?stem=all returns every node ordered by created_at. ?root=<hash> restricts turns to the subtree rooted at that node.

parameters.description: Session id (UUID)
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Which turns to return: longest (default) or all
parameters.name: stem

parameters.description: Restrict turns to the subtree rooted at this node hash
parameters.name: root

501.description: Sessions not supported by this backend

get.description: The raw layer's wire log: one row per captured call or transcript push, identity and sizes only. `source` distinguishes what crossed the wire from what the harness pushed as its own account.
get.summary: List a session's raw capture log (operator)

parameters.description: Session id (UUID)
parameters.name: id

get.description: Returns the session's user-visible turns as traces with nested spans (llm calls, tools, subagents, shadow calls, injected context) and dataflow links. Cross-trace links (compaction seams) are at the response top level.

parameters.description: Session id (UUID)
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Span payload mode: full (default) or preview (strings truncated; fetch the span endpoint for full payloads)
parameters.name: payload

501.description: Span traces not supported by this backend

get.description: Returns every captured node for the session typed by node_kind, with spine links, subagent fork/rejoin edges, and offshoot attachments (permission verdicts, web summaries) anchored to the tool_use they relate to.
get.summary: Get a session's reconciled conversation tree

parameters.description: Session id (UUID)
parameters.name: id

501.description: Sessions not supported by this backend

get.description: Returns counts plus cost / token / duration / tool-call / completed-count totals for the window. On span-projection backends the numbers are trace-grain rollup sums (delta-only usage, agent time = sum of trace durations) so they agree with the session and trace views; turn_count counts traces and stem_count is omitted. Supplying any legacy per-node filter (project / agent_name / model / provider) forces the legacy node-layer aggregate, whose sums re-bill re-sent history and whose duration is wall-clock MAX-MIN (PCC-514).

parameters.description: Filter by project name (forces the legacy node-layer aggregate)
parameters.name: project

parameters.description: Filter by agent name (forces the legacy node-layer aggregate)
parameters.name: agent_name

parameters.description: Filter by model name (forces the legacy node-layer aggregate)
parameters.name: model

parameters.description: Filter by provider name (forces the legacy node-layer aggregate)
parameters.name: provider

parameters.description: Only include records at or after this RFC3339 timestamp
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Only include records before or at this RFC3339 timestamp
parameters.name: until

get.description: Returns paginated per-stem summaries including derived labels, status, token counts, cost, duration, and truncation metadata.
get.summary: List stems (Merkle leaf chains) with aggregates

parameters.description: Filter by project name
parameters.name: project

parameters.description: Filter by agent name
parameters.name: agent_name

parameters.description: Filter by model name
parameters.name: model

parameters.description: Filter by provider name
parameters.name: provider

parameters.description: Only include stems updated at or after this RFC3339 timestamp
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Only include stems updated before or at this RFC3339 timestamp
parameters.name: until

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous response
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Maximum number of stems to return
parameters.name: limit

500.description: Failed to list or summarize stems

get.description: Returns the ancestry chain of a single Merkle leaf in chronological order (root first). When depth is provided, only the last N turns are returned while the full chain depth is still reported.

parameters.description: Stem head (leaf) hash
parameters.name: hash

parameters.description: Maximum number of most-recent turns to include
parameters.name: depth

get.description: Returns a graph-shaped projection of the Merkle DAG around a node hash (the same ancestry returned by GET /v1/stems/{hash}). scope=root loads the requested node's resolvable root and all descendants, scope=branch loads the ancestry plus descendants of the requested node, and scope=ancestry loads only the parent chain.

parameters.description: Node (stem head) hash
parameters.name: hash

parameters.description: Graph scope: root, branch, or ancestry
parameters.name: scope

parameters.description: Maximum number of graph nodes to include
parameters.name: max_nodes

400.description: Missing hash or invalid query parameters

get.description: Returns turn headers for a session — no span payloads. Fetch GET /v1/traces/{trace_id} per turn for spans and links.

parameters.description: Session id (UUID)
parameters.name: session_id

get.description: Returns one user-visible turn: its spans nested by parent_span_id and its dataflow links (links touching other traces included).
get.summary: Get one trace with spans and links

parameters.description: Trace id
parameters.name: trace_id

parameters.description: Span payload mode: full (default) or preview (strings truncated; fetch the span endpoint for full payloads)
parameters.name: payload

get.description: The payload drill-in: one span's complete input/output content.
get.summary: Get one span with full payloads

parameters.description: Trace id
parameters.name: trace_id

parameters.description: Span id
parameters.name: span_id

node_kind.description:
NodeKind / ParentToolUseID surface the derived semantic typing
(additive; empty for nodes that predate the deriver).

branch_points.description: BranchPoints names included nodes with more than one child in storage.

cycle_detected.description: CycleDetected is true when storage guarded the ancestry walk out of a cycle.

hash.description: Hash is the session/node hash requested by the caller.

leaves.description: Leaves names included nodes that have no children in storage.

links.description: Links contains parent -> child edges between included nodes.

missing_parent.description: MissingParent names the unresolved parent hash when the ancestry is incomplete.

node_limit.description: NodeLimit is the maximum number of nodes the server will include.

nodes.description: Nodes is the flat node list consumed by graph visualizers.

root_hash.description: RootHash is the top-most resolvable node included in the response.

scope.description: Scope describes which portion of the graph was loaded: root, branch, or ancestry.

truncated.description: Truncated is true when the graph hit NodeLimit or the ancestry chain is incomplete.

attachments.description:
Attachments link offshoot calls to the tool_use they relate to:
permission checks to the judged action, web summaries to their
fetch/search.

forks.description:
Forks are subagent threads: a chain whose root carries the Task
tool_use that spawned it. SourceID is the node holding the
tool_use block; RejoinID the node holding its tool_result.

kind_counts.description: KindCounts summarizes the session's call mix.

roots.description: Roots are the parentless chain roots, main thread first.

tasks.description:
Tasks is the session's task list, folded from the TaskCreate /
TaskUpdate tool calls across every thread — the checklist of
record with each task's final status.

metrics.description:
Metrics is always an object on the wire — the contract fixture
pins {} for usage-less spans (agent/tool/event), and the console
schema requires it.

seq.description:
Seq is the span's presentation ordinal within its trace; spans
arrive sorted by it. start_ns cannot order spans inside one llm
call (parallel tool batches share an instant).

snippet.description: Snippet previews the matched span's delta-only text.

user_prompt.description: UserPrompt is the prompt of the turn (trace) the span belongs to.

stem_count.description:
StemCount is a node-layer (Merkle leaf) concept with no span
equivalent; it is only present on the legacy fallback path.

depth.description:
Depth is the total number of turns in the full ancestry of Hash.
When the client passes ?depth=N, the Turns array may contain fewer
than Depth items.

harness_id.description:
HarnessID and HarnessSessionID identify the upstream agent session when
session tracking metadata is available. For Claude Code, HarnessID is
"claude" and HarnessSessionID is Claude's session id.

hash.description: Hash is the head of the returned chain (== the requested hash).

truncated.description:
Truncated is true when the ancestry walk stopped at a parent_hash
that could not be resolved in the current store. MissingParent
names that hash. This is an expected edge case on stores that
trim older data, merge foreign content, or offload history to
another source — not an error.

turns.description:
Turns contains the chain in chronological order (root-first).
When ?depth=N is supplied, only the last N turns (head + N-1 ancestors)
are returned, still in chronological order.

main_input_tokens.description:
Main* counts conversation-spine calls only; Total − Main is the
harness's shadow spend on the turn.

response_preview.description:
ResponsePreview is the derive-time fold of the closing
conversation-spine llm call's text output — the answer line for
collapsed turn cards, so summary consumers never need spans.

content.description:
Content is the node's full content blocks (raw, as captured) so
conversation renderers can format turns without a second fetch.

tool_uses.description:
ToolUses lists the tool_use blocks this node carries (assistant
turns), so clients can anchor forks/attachments without parsing
content.

verdict.description:
Verdict is set on permission-check response nodes: the monitor's
disposition for the judged action.

content.description:
Content (type="web_search_tool_result" and other server-tool results) -
the raw result payload Anthropic returns inline on the block, captured
verbatim as JSON so the variable result-object shapes survive without
imposing a schema. ToolResultID links it to the paired server_tool_use.

thinking.description:
Thinking (type="thinking") - Anthropic extended-thinking blocks.
Anthropic emits thinking as content_block_delta frames with type
"thinking_delta" followed by a "signature_delta" that authenticates the
block. Consumers treat Thinking as opaque text; the signature is persisted
so downstream tooling can verify integrity.

tool_result_id.description: Tool result (type="tool_result") - result from tool execution

tool_use_id.description: Tool use (type="tool_use") - assistant requesting tool execution

prompt_duration_ns.description:
PromptDurationNs is provider-reported prompt-evaluation time, in
nanoseconds. Populated by providers that surface it (currently Ollama
only); left at zero otherwise.

total_duration_ns.description:
TotalDurationNs is the proxy-measured wall-clock time, in nanoseconds,
from when the proxy received the client request to when the upstream
response was fully assembled. Set uniformly by the proxy across providers
— Anthropic and OpenAI don't surface a duration field on the wire, and
Ollama's server-internal `total_duration` is intentionally overwritten so
aggregate stats compare apples to apples.

truncated.description:
Truncated is true when this summary was built from a partial chain
because an ancestor's parent_hash could not be resolved in the
current store. MissingParent names the hash that would have
continued the walk if the referenced node existed. Both fields are
informational — they signal that higher ancestors may live outside
this store (trimmed history, foreign chain, offloaded data) rather
than an error.
